data.reasoningText: The user wants to run the tests in this project using `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` with crash dump collection. For .NET test projects, crash dumps can be collected using the `--blame-crash` option which is part of the `dotnet test` blame data collector. This will generate a crash dump if the test host process crashes.

The command would be:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

This uses the built-in blame data collector to capture crash dumps. Let me run this for the user.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-6a456111a0474be9b0ef6844b3c2b14b && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command to run tests with crash dump collection is:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

This uses the built-in blame data collector. If the test host process crashes, a crash dump (`.dmp` file) will be written to the `TestResults` directory. You can also add `--blame-crash-dump-type full` for a full memory dump (default is "mini").
